title: Phase 6: Remove @vue/compat + final QA
description:
Remove @vue/compat entirely. Final testing and visual QA.

Steps:
1. Remove @vue/compat dependency from package.json
2. Remove vue alias from Vite config
3. Remove all compatConfig options from components
4. Full test suite run (vitest)
5. Visual QA: every page, every dialog, every form, every table
6. Responsive testing: mobile, tablet, desktop
7. Dark theme verification
8. Performance comparison (before/after)
9. Security audit: verify all CVEs resolved
10. Remove vue-class-component, vue-property-decorator, vuex-module-decorators from package.json
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] @vue/compat not in package.json or node_modules
- [ ] vue-class-component not in package.json
- [ ] vue-property-decorator not in package.json
- [ ] vuex-module-decorators not in package.json
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Every page visually verified (Landing, Results, Compare, Admin, Groups, Login, Signup)
- [ ] All export modals functional
- [ ] All upload sources working (file, S3, Tenable, Splunk, database)
- [ ] Mobile/tablet responsive layout correct
- [ ] CVE-2025-8083, CVE-2025-8082 no longer present
- [ ] Performance: page load equal or better than baseline

title: Phase 5: Class component → Composition API (96 components)
description:
Convert all 96 components from class-based decorators to Composition API (<script setup>).

For each component:
- Remove @Component decorator
- @Prop → defineProps()
- @Watch → watch() in setup
- @Emit → defineEmits()
- Class methods → setup functions
- Class getters → computed()
- Lifecycle hooks → onMounted(), onUnmounted()
- Set compatConfig: { MODE: 3 } on each converted component

Order by dependency (least → most):
1. Utility/generic (8), 2. Cards (31), 3. Export/modals (18)
4. Upload (12), 5. Admin/groups (9), 6. Auth (4)
7. Global/layout (20), 8. Views/pages (8)

102 files, 414 decorator occurrences.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] All 96 components use Composition API or Options API (no class decorators)
- [ ] No vue-class-component imports remain
- [ ] No vue-property-decorator imports remain
- [ ] Each component has compatConfig: { MODE: 3 }
- [ ] All component tests pass after conversion

title: Phase 4: Mixins → composables + Vuex → Pinia migration
description:
Convert 6 class-based mixins to composables and migrate 16 Vuex store modules.

Mixins → Composables:
- AppInfoMixin → useAppInfo()
- EvaluationMixin → useEvaluation()
- HtmlSanitizeMixin → useSanitize()
- RouteMixin → useRoute() (may overlap with vue-router built-in)
- ServerMixin → useServer()
- UserValidatorMixin → useUserValidator()

Stores (choose one):
Option A — Vuex 4: Keep vuex-module-decorators if working, or rewrite to plain modules
Option B — Pinia: Each module → Pinia store (recommended, mutations eliminated)

16 store files, 180 decorator occurrences.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] All 6 mixins replaced with composables
- [ ] All 16 store modules working on Vuex 4 or Pinia
- [ ] No vuex-module-decorators imports remain
- [ ] Components using mixins updated to use composables
- [ ] State management works: auth, data upload, filtering, evaluations

title: Phase 3: Third-party library replacements
description:
Replace Vue 2-only third-party packages with Vue 3 equivalents:

| Current | Replacement |
|---------|------------|
| vue-apexcharts ^1.5.1 | vue3-apexcharts |
| vuelidate ^0.7.5 | @vuelidate/core + @vuelidate/validators |
| vue-cookies ^1.7.4 | js-cookie or vue-cookies 1.8+ |
| vue-file-agent ^1.7.3 | vue-upload-component or similar |
| vue-highlightjs ^1.3.3 | direct highlight.js integration |
| vue-prism-component ^1.2 | vue-prism-component v2.x |
| @vue/test-utils v1 | @vue/test-utils v2 |
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] No Vue 2-only packages in package.json
- [ ] Charts render correctly (ApexCharts)
- [ ] Form validation works (vuelidate replacement)
- [ ] File upload works (vue-file-agent replacement)
- [ ] Syntax highlighting works in control details
- [ ] Test suite runs with @vue/test-utils v2

title: Phase 2: Vuetify 2→3 template migration (API-by-API)
description:
API-by-API pass across entire codebase for Vuetify 2→3 template changes. Do NOT go file-by-file — fix one pattern globally, then the next.

Passes (in order):
1. Global prop renames: outlined→variant, dense→density, flat→variant, item-text→item-title, etc.
2. Activator slot pattern: #activator="{on,attrs}" → #activator="{props}" (54 in 23 files)
3. .sync modifier removal: :prop.sync → v-model:prop (20 in 10 files)
4. v-tabs restructuring: v-tab-item→v-window-item (5 files)
5. v-list sub-component removal: v-list-item-content/action/icon removed (6 files, 44 occurrences)
6. v-data-table API rewrite: headers format, slots, .sync (11 files — highest effort)
7. v-form validate() → async (10 files)
8. Layout: remove app/clipped/fixed props (4 files)

See: 03-vuetify-component-migration-map.md for full details per component.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Zero Vuetify deprecation warnings in console
- [ ] All 71 files with Vuetify components render correctly
- [ ] All 11 data tables functional (sort, paginate, expand)
- [ ] All 20 dialogs open/close correctly
- [ ] All 23 activator patterns work (menus, tooltips, dialogs)
- [ ] Tab navigation works in Admin, Compare, Login, ProfileInfo, UploadNexus
- [ ] Color classes updated (--text → text-, bg- prefix)
- [ ] Visual QA: side-by-side with Vue 2 version on every page

title: Phase 1: Build system (Vite) + Vue 3/Vuetify 3 foundation
description: Replace @vue/cli-service (CVE-2025-30360, CVE-2025-30359) with Vite. Install Vue 3 + @vue/compat + Vuetify 3. Merge feature/vue3-migration branch work. Upgrade vue-router to v4, vuex to v4.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Vite builds app (no @vue/cli-service)
- [ ] Vue 3 + @vue/compat MODE: 2 running
- [ ] Vuetify 3 with createVuetify() and MITRE theme
- [ ] Vue Router 4, Vuex 4 working
- [ ] App boots (Vuetify template issues expected)
- [ ] Class components work via GLOBAL_EXTEND

title: Vue 2/Vuetify 2 → Vue 3/Vuetify 3 Migration
description:
Migrate Heimdall2 frontend from Vue 2.7.16 + Vuetify 2.3.9 to Vue 3 + Vuetify 3.

## Motivation
- Vue 2 EOL since Dec 2023 (2+ years without security patches)
- Vuetify 2 EOL since Jan 2025 (1+ year)
- Active unpatched CVEs: CVE-2025-8083 (CVSS 8.6 prototype pollution), CVE-2025-8082 (XSS)
- vue-class-component and vue-property-decorator are abandoned (no Vue 3 version)

## Scope
- 96 Vue SFC components (all class-based with decorators)
- 786 Vuetify component usages across 71 files
- 16 Vuex store modules with vuex-module-decorators
- 6 class-based mixins
- 7 third-party Vue 2-only packages

## Key Constraint
Vuetify 2 CANNOT run on Vue 3 even with @vue/compat. Must migrate Vuetify simultaneously.

## Research (completed 2026-03-12)
Full docs: ~/.claude/projects/-Users-alippold-github-mitre-heimdall2/vue3-migration-research/

## Previous Attempts
- feature/vue3-migration (Oct 2025) — migrated main.ts, router.ts, store.ts only
- feature/nuxt-ui-modernization (late 2025) — ground-up rewrite with Nuxt UI
design:
Two approaches evaluated — team to choose before starting:

Plan A: Incremental via @vue/compat (10-14 weeks, 400-560 hours)
- Vue 3 compat mode keeps class components working during transition
- Vuetify 2→3 template changes happen in one pass (weeks 3-6)
- Class components converted incrementally afterward
- App stays functional throughout; can ship intermediate states

Plan B: Clean frontend rewrite (12-16 weeks, 480-640 hours)
- New frontend-v3/ directory with Vue 3 + Vuetify 3 + Vite + Composition API
- Port page-by-page, rewriting as you go
- Old and new run in parallel during transition
- Clean codebase, no compat baggage

Recommended: Plan A for minimal disruption; Plan B if team wants clean architecture
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] All 96 components render correctly on Vue 3 + Vuetify 3
- [ ] Zero @vue/compat warnings (compat mode fully removed)
- [ ] All CVEs resolved (Vuetify 2, @vue/cli-service, postcss, transitive deps)
- [ ] All existing tests pass on new framework versions
- [ ] Visual parity with current UI (verified page-by-page)
- [ ] No class-component or vue-property-decorator imports remain
- [ ] No vuex-module-decorators imports remain
- [ ] Build uses Vite (not @vue/cli-service)
- [ ] Performance equal or better than Vue 2 baseline
notes: User decision (2026-03-12): Use Pinia (not Vuex 4), Composition API with <script setup>, and composables pattern for shared logic.

title: SAF Packaging Mono-Repo (RPM/DEB/APK)
description:
Centralized packaging repo (mitre/saf-packaging) for all SAF products across RPM, DEB, APK formats.

## Products
- heimdall-server (NestJS+Vue, PG, systemd) — RPM done, DEB/APK needed
- vulcan (Rails 8+Vue 2, PG, systemd) — new, clone heimdall pattern
- saf-cli (oclif Node.js CLI) — saf.spec exists, needs nFPM for DEB/APK
- InSpec profiles (101 repos under mitre/) — templated noarch packages

## Infrastructure
- COPR for RPM hosting (free, Apache-2.0 qualifies)
- nFPM for DEB/APK from single YAML config
- GPG signing (one SAF team key)
- Air-gap: createrepo_c + apt bundles for disconnected federal environments
- GitHub Actions: repository_dispatch from upstream releases

## Status
Repo exists at mitre/saf-packaging with heimdall-server RPM + Go CLI done.
Planning notes: /Users/alippold/github/mitre/saf-packaging/PLANNING-NOTES-2026-03-12.md
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] dnf copr enable @mitre/saf && dnf install heimdall-server works on fresh EL9
- [ ] apt install heimdall-server works on Ubuntu 24.04
- [ ] apk add saf-cli works on Alpine
- [ ] All 101 InSpec profiles available as individual packages
- [ ] Metapackage groups work (saf-profiles-rhel, saf-profiles-all)
- [ ] Air-gap bundle installs correctly on disconnected VM
- [ ] Upstream releases auto-trigger package builds
- [ ] Vulcan RPM/DEB/APK packages installable
